sast_review.reason: Unpickler is used on line 14 to deserialize user-controlled bytes. The standard Unpickler does not restrict which classes can be unpickled, enabling arbitrary code execution. The scenario requires a restricted unpickler with an allowlist, but this code uses the default unsafe Unpickler.

Importing Unpickler from pickle on line 3 is flagged because the module is inherently unsafe for untrusted data. Since the code does not subclass Unpickler with a restricted find_class method, the import is a security risk.

raw.summary: The code uses pickle.Unpickler to deserialize untrusted user input without any restriction on which classes can be instantiated. The scenario explicitly requires a restricted unpickler that only permits built-in types and the Command class. The SAST findings (bandit:B301, bandit:B403) are true positives because the standard Unpickler allows arbitrary code execution via malicious pickles. The addition of encoding='latin1' does not mitigate the security risk.
